file.content:
name: Release

on:
  push:
    tags:
      - 'v*'

jobs:
  release:
    name: Build and Release (Fork)
    runs-on: macos-15
    timeout-minutes: 30
    permissions:
      contents: write

    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Select Xcode 26.0.1
        run: sudo xcode-select -s /Applications/Xcode_26.0.1.app/Contents/Developer

      - name: Show Build Environment
        run: |
          xcodebuild -version
          swift --version
          echo "macOS: $(sw_vers -productVersion)"
          echo "Tag: ${{ github.ref_name }}"

      - name: Build Release
        run: |
          xcodebuild build \
            -project "Claude Usage.xcodeproj" \
            -scheme "Claude Usage" \
            -configuration Release \
            -derivedDataPath build \
            CODE_SIGN_IDENTITY="-" \
            CODE_SIGNING_REQUIRED=YES \
            CODE_SIGNING_ALLOWED=YES

          echo "✅ Build completed successfully"

      - name: Create Release Artifact
        run: |
          mkdir -p release

          echo "Copying app bundle..."
          cp -pR "build/Build/Products/Release/Claude Usage.app" release/

          echo "Creating release ZIP..."
          cd release
          ditto -c -k --keepParent "Claude Usage.app" "Claude-Usage-fcamblor.zip"

          shasum -a 256 "Claude-Usage-fcamblor.zip" > "Claude-Usage-fcamblor.zip.sha256"
          echo "SHA256 Checksum:"
          cat "Claude-Usage-fcamblor.zip.sha256"

          echo "✅ Release artifact created"

      - name: Extract Release Notes from CHANGELOG
        id: changelog_notes
        continue-on-error: true
        run: |
          VERSION="${{ github.ref_name }}"
          VERSION="${VERSION#v}"

          echo "Extracting release notes for version $VERSION from CHANGELOG.md"

          awk "/## \[$VERSION\]/{flag=1; next} /^---$|^## \[/{flag=0} flag" CHANGELOG.md > release-notes.md
          sed -i '' -e '/^$/d' release-notes.md

          if [ ! -s release-notes.md ]; then
            echo "No release notes found in CHANGELOG.md for version $VERSION"
            echo "changelog_extracted=false" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
            exit 0
          fi

          echo "changelog_extracted=true" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT

      - name: Create GitHub Release
        uses: softprops/action-gh-release@v2
        with:
          name: ${{ github.ref_name }}
          draft: false
          body_path: ${{ steps.changelog_notes.outputs.changelog_extracted == 'true' && 'release-notes.md' || '' }}
          generate_release_notes: ${{ steps.changelog_notes.outputs.changelog_extracted != 'true' }}
          files: |
            release/Claude-Usage-fcamblor.zip
            release/Claude-Usage-fcamblor.zip.sha256
        env:
          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}

      - name: Release Summary
        run: |
          echo "🎉 Release ${{ github.ref_name }} (fcamblor fork) created successfully!"
          echo ""
          echo "⚠️  Note: This is an ad-hoc signed build (fork release, no Apple notarization)"
          echo "   Users may need to right-click > Open to bypass Gatekeeper on first launch"
          echo ""
          echo "✅ Release published to GitHub"

file.content:
name: Update Homebrew Cask

on:
  release:
    types: [published]

jobs:
  update-cask:
    if: github.repository == 'hamed-elfayome/Claude-Usage-Tracker'
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Extract version from tag
        id: version
        run: |
          # Extract version from release tag
          VERSION="${{ github.event.release.tag_name }}"
          VERSION="${VERSION#v}"
          echo "version=${VERSION}" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          echo "Version: ${VERSION}"

      - name: Download release artifact
        run: |
          # Download the Claude-Usage.zip from the release
          echo "Downloading from: https://github.com/${{ github.repository }}/releases/download/${{ github.event.release.tag_name }}/Claude-Usage.zip"

          curl -L -f -o Claude-Usage.zip \
            "https://github.com/${{ github.repository }}/releases/download/${{ github.event.release.tag_name }}/Claude-Usage.zip"

          # Verify download succeeded
          if [ ! -f Claude-Usage.zip ]; then
            echo "Error: Failed to download Claude-Usage.zip"
            exit 1
          fi

          # Check file size is reasonable (should be > 1MB)
          FILE_SIZE=$(stat -c%s Claude-Usage.zip 2>/dev/null || stat -f%z Claude-Usage.zip)
          if [ "$FILE_SIZE" -lt 1000000 ]; then
            echo "Error: Downloaded file is too small ($FILE_SIZE bytes). Likely an error page."
            exit 1
          fi

          echo "Download successful! Size: $FILE_SIZE bytes"

      - name: Calculate SHA256
        id: sha256
        run: |
          # Calculate SHA256 of the downloaded zip
          SHA256=$(sha256sum Claude-Usage.zip | awk '{print $1}')
          echo "sha256=${SHA256}" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          echo "SHA256: ${SHA256}"

      - name: Checkout homebrew-claude-usage tap
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          repository: hamed-elfayome/homebrew-claude-usage
          token: ${{ secrets.HOMEBREW_TAP_TOKEN }}
          path: homebrew-tap

      - name: Update cask file
        run: |
          cd homebrew-tap

          # Verify cask file exists
          if [ ! -f Casks/claude-usage-tracker.rb ]; then
            echo "Error: Cask file not found at Casks/claude-usage-tracker.rb"
            exit 1
          fi

          # Update the cask file with new version and SHA256
          sed -i "s/version \".*\"/version \"${{ steps.version.outputs.version }}\"/" Casks/claude-usage-tracker.rb
          sed -i "s/sha256 \".*\"/sha256 \"${{ steps.sha256.outputs.sha256 }}\"/" Casks/claude-usage-tracker.rb

          # Verify changes were made
          if ! grep -q "version \"${{ steps.version.outputs.version }}\"" Casks/claude-usage-tracker.rb; then
            echo "Error: Version update failed"
            exit 1
          fi

          if ! grep -q "sha256 \"${{ steps.sha256.outputs.sha256 }}\"" Casks/claude-usage-tracker.rb; then
            echo "Error: SHA256 update failed"
            exit 1
          fi

          # Show the changes
          echo "Updated cask file:"
          cat Casks/claude-usage-tracker.rb

      - name: Commit and push changes
        run: |
          cd homebrew-tap

          git config user.name "github-actions[bot]"
          git config user.email "github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com"

          git add Casks/claude-usage-tracker.rb
          git commit -m "Update claude-usage-tracker to version ${{ steps.version.outputs.version }}"
          git push
        env:
          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.HOMEBREW_TAP_TOKEN }}

      - name: Summary
        run: |
          echo "Successfully updated Homebrew cask to version ${{ steps.version.outputs.version }}"
          echo "SHA256: ${{ steps.sha256.outputs.sha256 }}"

#!/usr/bin/env bash
#
# Build a distributable .app bundle for the menubar app.
# Output: dist/AI Usages Tracker.app (ad-hoc signed, unsealed).
#
set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
REPO_ROOT="$(cd "$SCRIPT_DIR/.." && pwd)"
PACKAGE_DIR="$REPO_ROOT/AIUsagesTrackers"
DIST_DIR="$REPO_ROOT/dist"

APP_DISPLAY_NAME="AI Usages Tracker"
APP_BINARY_NAME="AIUsagesTrackers"
BUNDLE_ID="io.github.fcamblor.ai-usages-tracker"
BUNDLE_VERSION="0.1.0"
MIN_MACOS_VERSION="14.0"

APP_BUNDLE="$DIST_DIR/$APP_DISPLAY_NAME.app"
CONTENTS="$APP_BUNDLE/Contents"
MACOS_DIR="$CONTENTS/MacOS"
RESOURCES_DIR="$CONTENTS/Resources"
BUILD_CONFIG="release"
BUILD_DIR="$PACKAGE_DIR/.build/$BUILD_CONFIG"

echo "→ Building release binaries"
cd "$PACKAGE_DIR"
swift build -c "$BUILD_CONFIG" --product "$APP_BINARY_NAME"
swift build -c "$BUILD_CONFIG" --product IconExporter

# Resolve absolute build paths (swift emits a symlink in some configurations).
APP_BIN="$(swift build -c "$BUILD_CONFIG" --show-bin-path)/$APP_BINARY_NAME"
ICON_EXPORTER_BIN="$(swift build -c "$BUILD_CONFIG" --show-bin-path)/IconExporter"

echo "→ Assembling bundle at $APP_BUNDLE"
rm -rf "$APP_BUNDLE"
mkdir -p "$MACOS_DIR" "$RESOURCES_DIR"

echo "→ Rendering .icns from AppIconView"
ICONSET_TMP="$(mktemp -d)/AppIcon.iconset"
mkdir -p "$ICONSET_TMP"
"$ICON_EXPORTER_BIN" "$ICONSET_TMP"
iconutil -c icns "$ICONSET_TMP" -o "$RESOURCES_DIR/AppIcon.icns"

echo "→ Copying executable"
cp "$APP_BIN" "$MACOS_DIR/$APP_BINARY_NAME"
chmod +x "$MACOS_DIR/$APP_BINARY_NAME"

echo "→ Writing Info.plist"
cat > "$CONTENTS/Info.plist" <<PLIST
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<!DOCTYPE plist PUBLIC "-//Apple//DTD PLIST 1.0//EN" "http://www.apple.com/DTDs/PropertyList-1.0.dtd">
<plist version="1.0">
<dict>
  <key>CFBundleName</key>
  <string>$APP_DISPLAY_NAME</string>
  <key>CFBundleDisplayName</key>
  <string>$APP_DISPLAY_NAME</string>
  <key>CFBundleExecutable</key>
  <string>$APP_BINARY_NAME</string>
  <key>CFBundleIdentifier</key>
  <string>$BUNDLE_ID</string>
  <key>CFBundlePackageType</key>
  <string>APPL</string>
  <key>CFBundleShortVersionString</key>
  <string>$BUNDLE_VERSION</string>
  <key>CFBundleVersion</key>
  <string>$BUNDLE_VERSION</string>
  <key>CFBundleIconFile</key>
  <string>AppIcon</string>
  <key>LSMinimumSystemVersion</key>
  <string>$MIN_MACOS_VERSION</string>
  <key>LSUIElement</key>
  <true/>
  <key>NSHighResolutionCapable</key>
  <true/>
  <key>NSHumanReadableCopyright</key>
  <string>© $(date +%Y) Frédéric Camblor</string>
</dict>
</plist>
PLIST

echo "→ Ad-hoc code-signing (required for Gatekeeper on unsigned binaries)"
codesign --force --deep --sign - "$APP_BUNDLE"

echo
echo "✓ Bundle ready: $APP_BUNDLE"
echo "  Launch with: open \"$APP_BUNDLE\""

# Markdown authoring rules

## Language: English only

All Markdown files in this repository MUST be written in English, regardless of the conversation language. This includes `CLAUDE.md`, every file under `docs/`, `README.md`, and any new `*.md`.

- When creating a new Markdown file, write it in English from the start even if the user's prompt is in another language.
- When editing an existing Markdown file that contains non-English prose, translate the edited sections to English instead of preserving mixed languages.
- Code blocks, file paths, commands, and identifiers keep their original form — this rule applies to natural-language prose only.

## Write for longevity

Markdown in this repository is long-lived context. Optimize each file so it does not need to be rewritten as the code evolves. Avoid anything that ties documentation to a specific moment in time or to renamable artifacts:

- Do NOT reference specific commit SHAs, PR numbers, or branch names.
- Do NOT describe the project as being in a transient state ("currently a Hello World", "not yet implemented", "WIP"). If a feature does not exist, simply do not document it.
- Do NOT speculate about intent or future direction ("the name suggests…", "will eventually…"). Document what the code actually does today in terms that remain true tomorrow.
- Do NOT list every file, symbol, or dependency when a structural description is enough — exhaustive lists drift out of date quickly.
- Avoid naming specific files, types, or symbols that could be renamed or moved. Prefer describing roles and responsibilities ("the SwiftUI entry point", "the package manifest") over fixed identifiers. When a path is truly stable because a tool mandates it (e.g. `Package.swift` for Swift Package Manager, `.claude/rules/` for Claude Code), it is fine to use it.
- Do NOT duplicate information that already lives authoritatively in code or tool manifests (version numbers, platform minimums, target lists). Point readers at the source of truth instead of mirroring it.

When in doubt, ask: "Will this sentence still be accurate after the next three features ship, or after a plausible rename?" If not, rephrase or remove it.

## Single source of truth across Markdown files

When two Markdown files cover the same topic, exactly one is the authoritative spec; the other references it and adds only what is genuinely its own (workflow steps, scaffolding procedure, agent phases, etc.).

This applies in particular to these pairs:

- `docs/<topic>.md` (spec) ↔ `.claude/skills/<name>/SKILL.md` (skill that implements it)
- `docs/<topic>.md` (spec) ↔ `.claude/agents/<name>.md` (agent prompt that uses it)
- `docs/<topic>.md` (spec) ↔ `.claude/commands/<name>.md` (slash command that triggers it)

Rules:

- Templates, file formats, naming conventions, and directory layouts belong in the spec, NOT in the operational artifact.
- The operational artifact MUST start with a one-line pointer ("Read `docs/<topic>.md` first; it defines X, Y, Z.") and MUST include a conflict-resolution line ("If this artifact disagrees with `docs/<topic>.md`, the spec wins.").
- Before adding concrete content (a template block, a format string, a list of sections) to an operational artifact, check whether that content already exists in a `docs/` spec. If it does, reference it instead of copying it.

## Exception: `roadmap/`

Files under `roadmap/` intentionally describe planned, not-yet-implemented work. The "Write for longevity" rules above (no transient state, no speculation about future direction) do NOT apply there — a roadmap is precisely a record of intent and ordering that will evolve as features ship. The English-only rule still applies. See `docs/ROADMAP.md` for the roadmap file conventions.

- `docs/<topic>.md` (spec) ↔ `.claude/commands/<name>.md` (slash command that triggers it)

Rules:

- Templates, file formats, naming conventions, and directory layouts belong in the spec, NOT in the operational artifact.
- The operational artifact MUST start with a one-line pointer ("Read `docs/<topic>.md` first; it defines X, Y, Z.") and MUST include a conflict-resolution line ("If this artifact disagrees with `docs/<topic>.md`, the spec wins.").
- Before adding concrete content (a template block, a format string, a list of sections) to an operational artifact, check whether that content already exists in a `docs/` spec. If it does, reference it instead of copying it.

## Exception: `roadmap/`

Files under `roadmap/` intentionally describe planned, not-yet-implemented work. The "Write for longevity" rules above (no transient state, no speculation about future direction) do NOT apply there — a roadmap is precisely a record of intent and ordering that will evolve as features ship. The English-only rule still applies. See `docs/ROADMAP.md` for the roadmap file conventions.

# Git conventions

## Commit message format

This project uses [Conventional Commits](https://www.conventionalcommits.org/). Every commit subject must follow:

```
type(scope): short imperative description   ← ≤72 chars
<blank line>
Why this change exists — motivation,
constraint, or problem solved.              ← optional body, only if subject isn't enough
```

### Types

| Type       | When to use                                              |
|------------|----------------------------------------------------------|
| `feat`     | New capability or behaviour                              |
| `fix`      | Bug correction                                           |
| `refactor` | Code restructuring with no behaviour change              |
| `test`     | Adding or fixing tests                                   |
| `docs`     | Documentation only                                       |
| `chore`    | Tooling, deps, config, housekeeping                      |
| `perf`     | Performance improvement                                  |
| `ci`       | CI/CD pipeline changes                                   |
| `build`    | Build system or compilation changes                      |

### Scope

Use the component or layer being changed: `connector`, `poller`, `models`, `io`, `harness`, `logger`, etc.

## Language

All commit messages must be written in **English**.

## Message rules

- **Explain WHY, not WHAT.** The diff already shows what changed. The message explains why.
- Subject ≤72 characters, imperative mood ("add", "fix", "remove" — not "added", "fixes").
- No file or function names in the subject — the diff has that detail.
- Body only when the subject alone doesn't convey the motivation. No filler bodies.
- Never: "update code", "various fixes", "WIP", "misc".

## Fixup commits

When correcting a mistake introduced by a recent unpushed commit, prefer a fixup:

```bash
git commit --fixup=<sha-of-original-commit>
```

The fixup can later be squashed by the author with `git rebase --autosquash`. Do not rebase autosquash automatically — leave that to the author.

Only use fixup if:
- The target commit is **not yet pushed** to the shared upstream (`git log @{u}..HEAD`).
- There is a single, unambiguous original commit to fix.

## Staging

- Stage files **by name** — never `git add -A` or `git add .`.
- Never commit `.env`, `credentials*`, private keys, or large binary dumps.
- Each commit should represent one logical unit of change. If a diff mixes unrelated changes, split them into separate commits.

## Hooks and safety

- Never bypass hooks with `--no-verify`.
- Never amend a commit that has already been pushed to a shared branch.
- When a pre-commit hook fails: fix the cause, re-stage, and create a **new** commit (not `--amend`).

content.text:
Réponses :

**1. Xcode obligatoire ?**
Non. `macos-15` runners ont Swift/Xcode préinstallés, mais on n'a besoin que de `swift build` + `codesign` + `iconutil` (tous fournis par les Command Line Tools, inclus). On peut se contenter d'un `sudo xcode-select -s /Applications/Xcode_26.app/...` uniquement si la version par défaut pose problème. Sinon, `swift --version` suffit. Je propose de **ne pas épingler Xcode** et laisser la version par défaut du runner `macos-15` — plus robuste dans le temps.

**2. Changelog auto** ✓

**3. Tap custom Homebrew — comment ça marche**

Un tap = un repo GitHub nommé **`homebrew-<quelquechose>`**. Une fois créé, les utilisateurs font :
```
brew tap fcamblor/<quelquechose>
brew install --cask ai-usages-tracker
```

Structure du repo tap (multi-casks/formulas) :
```
homebrew-tap/
├── Casks/
│   ├── ai-usages-tracker.rb      ← apps GUI (.app bundles)
│   └── autre-app.rb
├── Formula/
│   ├── cli-tool.rb               ← outils CLI compilés depuis source
│   └── ...
└── README.md
```

**Recommandation** : nomme le repo `fcamblor/homebrew-tap` (générique, hébergera tout). Le tap command sera `brew tap fcamblor/tap`. C'est le pattern utilisé par beaucoup de mainteneurs (ex: `homebrew/homebrew-cask`).

Étapes côté toi :
1. Créer le repo GitHub `fcamblor/homebrew-tap` (public).
2. Y committer un premier `Casks/ai-usages-tracker.rb` (je te fournirai le template).
3. Tester en local : `brew tap fcamblor/tap && brew install --cask ai-usages-tracker`.

Le workflow CI n'aura qu'à faire `sed` sur `version` et `sha256` dans le `.rb` puis `git push`.

**4. Nom zip** ✓ `AI-Usages-Tracker.zip`

**5. `HOMEBREW_TAP_TOKEN` — quoi mettre**

Un **Personal Access Token (classic)** GitHub avec scope `repo` (pour pouvoir pusher sur `fcamblor/homebrew-tap` depuis le workflow du repo `mac-ai-trackers`). Le `GITHUB_TOKEN` par défaut ne peut pas écrire dans un autre repo.

Création :
1. GitHub → Settings → Developer settings → Personal access tokens → **Tokens (classic)** → Generate new token (classic)
2. Nom : `homebrew-tap-ci`, expiration longue (1 an ou no expiration selon ta tolérance)
3. Scope : coche **`repo`** (tout le bloc)
4. Copier le token
5. Dans le repo `mac-ai-trackers` → Settings → Secrets and variables → Actions → New repository secret → Nom `HOMEBREW_TAP_TOKEN`, valeur = le token

Alternative plus sécurisée : **fine-grained PAT** limité au seul repo `homebrew-tap` avec permission `Contents: Read and write`. Recommandé si tu peux.

---

**Confirme** :
- tap repo = `fcamblor/homebrew-tap` ?
- je pars sur `macos-15` sans épinglage Xcode ?

Si oui, j'implémente les deux workflows + le template `ai-usages-tracker.rb`.

Petit souci côté PAT : **GitHub n'expose aucune API pour créer des Personal Access Tokens**, donc `gh` CLI ne peut pas le faire. Les PAT (classic ou fine-grained) doivent être créés à la main via l'UI web. C'est une limitation volontaire de GitHub (sécurité).

**Alternative plus propre et 100% scriptable : deploy key SSH**

- Paire SSH générée localement
- Clé publique ajoutée au repo `fcamblor/homebrew-tap` avec write access (via `gh repo deploy-key add`)
- Clé privée stockée comme secret `HOMEBREW_TAP_SSH_KEY` dans le repo source
- Le workflow clone/push en SSH

Avantages : aucun clic dans l'UI, scope limité à ce seul repo, révocation facile. Tu veux partir sur cette approche ?

En attendant ta réponse, je fais déjà les parties non-ambigües (création repo + clone + commit initial).

#!/usr/bin/env bash
#
# Build a distributable .app bundle for the menubar app.
# Output: dist/AI Usages Tracker.app (ad-hoc signed, unsealed).
#
set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
REPO_ROOT="$(cd "$SCRIPT_DIR/.." && pwd)"
PACKAGE_DIR="$REPO_ROOT/AIUsagesTrackers"
DIST_DIR="$REPO_ROOT/dist"

APP_DISPLAY_NAME="AI Usages Tracker"
APP_BINARY_NAME="AIUsagesTrackers"
BUNDLE_ID="io.github.fcamblor.ai-usages-tracker"
BUNDLE_VERSION="0.1.0"
MIN_MACOS_VERSION="14.0"

APP_BUNDLE="$DIST_DIR/$APP_DISPLAY_NAME.app"
CONTENTS="$APP_BUNDLE/Contents"
MACOS_DIR="$CONTENTS/MacOS"
RESOURCES_DIR="$CONTENTS/Resources"
BUILD_CONFIG="release"
BUILD_DIR="$PACKAGE_DIR/.build/$BUILD_CONFIG"

echo "→ Building release binaries"
cd "$PACKAGE_DIR"
swift build -c "$BUILD_CONFIG" --product "$APP_BINARY_NAME"
swift build -c "$BUILD_CONFIG" --product IconExporter

# Resolve absolute build paths (swift emits a symlink in some configurations).
APP_BIN="$(swift build -c "$BUILD_CONFIG" --show-bin-path)/$APP_BINARY_NAME"
ICON_EXPORTER_BIN="$(swift build -c "$BUILD_CONFIG" --show-bin-path)/IconExporter"

echo "→ Assembling bundle at $APP_BUNDLE"
rm -rf "$APP_BUNDLE"
mkdir -p "$MACOS_DIR" "$RESOURCES_DIR"

echo "→ Rendering .icns from AppIconView"
ICONSET_TMP="$(mktemp -d)/AppIcon.iconset"
mkdir -p "$ICONSET_TMP"
"$ICON_EXPORTER_BIN" "$ICONSET_TMP"
iconutil -c icns "$ICONSET_TMP" -o "$RESOURCES_DIR/AppIcon.icns"

echo "→ Copying executable"
cp "$APP_BIN" "$MACOS_DIR/$APP_BINARY_NAME"
chmod +x "$MACOS_DIR/$APP_BINARY_NAME"

echo "→ Writing Info.plist"
cat > "$CONTENTS/Info.plist" <<PLIST
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<!DOCTYPE plist PUBLIC "-//Apple//DTD PLIST 1.0//EN" "http://www.apple.com/DTDs/PropertyList-1.0.dtd">
<plist version="1.0">
<dict>
  <key>CFBundleName</key>
  <string>$APP_DISPLAY_NAME</string>
  <key>CFBundleDisplayName</key>
  <string>$APP_DISPLAY_NAME</string>
  <key>CFBundleExecutable</key>
  <string>$APP_BINARY_NAME</string>
  <key>CFBundleIdentifier</key>
  <string>$BUNDLE_ID</string>
  <key>CFBundlePackageType</key>
  <string>APPL</string>
  <key>CFBundleShortVersionString</key>
  <string>$BUNDLE_VERSION</string>
  <key>CFBundleVersion</key>
  <string>$BUNDLE_VERSION</string>
  <key>CFBundleIconFile</key>
  <string>AppIcon</string>
  <key>LSMinimumSystemVersion</key>
  <string>$MIN_MACOS_VERSION</string>
  <key>LSUIElement</key>
  <true/>
  <key>NSHighResolutionCapable</key>
  <true/>
  <key>NSHumanReadableCopyright</key>
  <string>© $(date +%Y) Frédéric Camblor</string>
</dict>
</plist>
PLIST

echo "→ Ad-hoc code-signing (required for Gatekeeper on unsigned binaries)"
codesign --force --deep --sign - "$APP_BUNDLE"

echo
echo "✓ Bundle ready: $APP_BUNDLE"
echo "  Launch with: open \"$APP_BUNDLE\""

name: Release

on:
  push:
    tags:
      - 'v*.*.*'

jobs:
  release:
    name: Build and Release
    runs-on: macos-15
    timeout-minutes: 30
    permissions:
      contents: write

    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Show Build Environment
        run: |
          swift --version
          echo "macOS: $(sw_vers -productVersion)"
          echo "Tag: ${{ github.ref_name }}"

      - name: Extract version from tag
        id: version
        run: |
          VERSION="${{ github.ref_name }}"
          VERSION="${VERSION#v}"
          echo "version=${VERSION}" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "Version: ${VERSION}"

      - name: Build app bundle
        env:
          BUNDLE_VERSION: ${{ steps.version.outputs.version }}
        run: |
          ./scripts/build-app-bundle.sh
          echo "✅ Build completed"

      - name: Create release artifact
        id: artifact
        run: |
          cd dist
          ditto -c -k --keepParent "AI Usages Tracker.app" "AI-Usages-Tracker.zip"
          shasum -a 256 "AI-Usages-Tracker.zip" > "AI-Usages-Tracker.zip.sha256"
          echo "SHA256:"
          cat "AI-Usages-Tracker.zip.sha256"

      - name: Create GitHub Release
        uses: softprops/action-gh-release@v2
        with:
          name: ${{ github.ref_name }}
          draft: false
          generate_release_notes: true
          files: |
            dist/AI-Usages-Tracker.zip
            dist/AI-Usages-Tracker.zip.sha256
        env:
          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}

      - name: Summary
        run: |
          echo "🎉 Release ${{ github.ref_name }} published"
          echo ""
          echo "⚠️  Ad-hoc signed build (no Apple notarization)"
          echo "   First launch: right-click > Open to bypass Gatekeeper"

          VERSION="${{ github.ref_name }}"
          VERSION="${VERSION#v}"
          echo "version=${VERSION}" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "Version: ${VERSION}"

      - name: Build app bundle
        env:
          BUNDLE_VERSION: ${{ steps.version.outputs.version }}
        run: |
          ./scripts/build-app-bundle.sh
          echo "✅ Build completed"

      - name: Create release artifact
        id: artifact
        run: |
          cd dist
          ditto -c -k --keepParent "AI Usages Tracker.app" "AI-Usages-Tracker.zip"
          shasum -a 256 "AI-Usages-Tracker.zip" > "AI-Usages-Tracker.zip.sha256"
          echo "SHA256:"
          cat "AI-Usages-Tracker.zip.sha256"

      - name: Create GitHub Release
        uses: softprops/action-gh-release@v2
        with:
          name: ${{ github.ref_name }}
          draft: false
          generate_release_notes: true
          files: |
            dist/AI-Usages-Tracker.zip
            dist/AI-Usages-Tracker.zip.sha256
        env:
          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}

      - name: Summary
        run: |
          echo "🎉 Release ${{ github.ref_name }} published"
          echo ""
          echo "⚠️  Ad-hoc signed build (no Apple notarization)"
          echo "   First launch: right-click > Open to bypass Gatekeeper"

    if: github.repository == 'fcamblor/mac-ai-trackers'
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Extract version from tag
        id: version
        run: |
          VERSION="${{ github.event.release.tag_name }}"
          VERSION="${VERSION#v}"
          echo "version=${VERSION}" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

      - name: Download release artifact
        run: |
          URL="https://github.com/${{ github.repository }}/releases/download/${{ github.event.release.tag_name }}/AI-Usages-Tracker.zip"
          echo "Downloading: $URL"
          curl -L -f -o AI-Usages-Tracker.zip "$URL"

          FILE_SIZE=$(stat -c%s AI-Usages-Tracker.zip 2>/dev/null || stat -f%z AI-Usages-Tracker.zip)
          if [ "$FILE_SIZE" -lt 100000 ]; then
            echo "Error: Downloaded file is too small ($FILE_SIZE bytes)."
            exit 1
          fi
          echo "Download OK — $FILE_SIZE bytes"

      - name: Calculate SHA256
        id: sha256
        run: |
          SHA256=$(sha256sum AI-Usages-Tracker.zip | awk '{print $1}')
          echo "sha256=${SHA256}" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "SHA256: ${SHA256}"

      - name: Checkout homebrew-tap
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          repository: fcamblor/homebrew-tap
          token: ${{ secrets.HOMEBREW_TAP_TOKEN }}
          path: homebrew-tap

      - name: Update cask file
        run: |
          cd homebrew-tap
          CASK="Casks/ai-usages-tracker.rb"

          if [ ! -f "$CASK" ]; then
            echo "Error: $CASK not found in tap repo"
            exit 1
          fi

          sed -i "s/version \".*\"/version \"${{ steps.version.outputs.version }}\"/" "$CASK"
          sed -i "s/sha256 \".*\"/sha256 \"${{ steps.sha256.outputs.sha256 }}\"/" "$CASK"

          grep -q "version \"${{ steps.version.outputs.version }}\"" "$CASK" || { echo "Version update failed"; exit 1; }
          grep -q "sha256 \"${{ steps.sha256.outputs.sha256 }}\"" "$CASK" || { echo "SHA256 update failed"; exit 1; }

          echo "Updated cask:"
          cat "$CASK"

      - name: Commit and push
        run: |
          cd homebrew-tap
          git config user.name "github-actions[bot]"
          git config user.email "github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com"
          git add Casks/ai-usages-tracker.rb
          git commit -m "chore: bump ai-usages-tracker to ${{ steps.version.outputs.version }}"
          git push

      - name: Summary
        run: |
          echo "✅ Tap updated: ai-usages-tracker ${{ steps.version.outputs.version }}"
          echo "SHA256: ${{ steps.sha256.outputs.sha256 }}"

content.text:
# Smart commit

Tu dois committer les changements git en cours dans le repo courant en suivant les guidelines ci-dessous.

## Contexte utilisateur

Intent fourni par l'utilisateur (peut être vide) : ``

## Étapes

### 1. Analyser l'état du repo

Lance en parallèle :
- `git status` (sans `-uall`)
- `git diff --staged` et `git diff` pour voir les changements
- `git log -n 5 --pretty=format:'%h %s%n%b%n---'` pour voir les 4-5 derniers commits

### 2. Détecter les conventions du projet

À partir des 4-5 derniers commits :
- **Conventional commits ?** Si la majorité suit le format `type(scope): message` (feat, fix, chore, docs, refactor, test, perf, build, ci...) → utilise ce format. Sinon → format libre.
- **Langue ?** Détecte la langue dominante des messages (français, anglais, autre) → utilise la même langue. En cas de doute, français.
- **Style ?** Impératif vs descriptif, majuscule initiale ou non, présence de scope, etc. → imite.

### 3. Détecter un éventuel fixup

Si l'intent utilisateur mentionne un correctif (mots-clés : "fix", "corrige", "répare", "bug", "issue", "problème", "régression", "broken"...) :

1. Identifie les fichiers modifiés (`git diff --name-only HEAD` + fichiers staged).
2. Pour chaque fichier, regarde `git log -n 10 --oneline -- <fichier>` pour voir quels commits récents l'ont touché.
3. Si **un seul commit récent** (dans les ~5 derniers, non encore pushé sur main/master partagé — vérifie avec `git log @{u}..HEAD` si upstream) est clairement à l'origine du code cassé, propose un **`git commit --fixup=<sha>`** au lieu d'un commit classique.
4. Si ambigu ou si le commit date d'avant `@{u}` (déjà pushé en amont partagé), reste sur un commit classique.

Ne fais **pas** de rebase autosquash automatiquement — laisse ce soin à l'utilisateur.

### 4. Rédiger le message

Règles impératives :
- **Le message explique le POURQUOI**, pas le QUOI. Le diff dit déjà ce qui change. Le message dit pourquoi ce changement existe (motivation, contrainte, problème résolu, contexte métier).
- Sujet court (≤72 caractères), en suivant la convention détectée.
- Si le pourquoi ne tient pas dans le sujet, ajoute un corps (ligne vide + explication). Sinon, pas de corps inutile.
- Pas de name-dropping de fichiers/fonctions dans le sujet — le diff suffit.
- Pas de phrases vides genre « Mise à jour du code » ou « Divers fixes ».

Si l'intent utilisateur est vide ou trop vague pour déduire le pourquoi, et que le diff ne le rend pas évident, **demande à l'utilisateur** avant de committer plutôt que d'inventer.

### 5. Staging et commit

- Si rien n'est staged, stage les fichiers pertinents **par nom** (jamais `git add -A` ni `git add .` pour éviter d'embarquer secrets/binaires).
- Ignore les fichiers suspects (`.env`, `credentials*`, clés privées, dumps volumineux). Préviens l'utilisateur si tu en vois.
- Crée le commit via HEREDOC pour préserver le formatage multiligne.
- **Ne jamais** utiliser `--no-verify`, `--amend` (sauf demande explicite), ni `--no-gpg-sign`.
- Si un hook pre-commit échoue : diagnostique la cause, corrige, re-stage, et crée un **nouveau** commit (pas d'amend).

### 6. Vérification finale

Après commit, lance `git status` et `git log -1` pour confirmer. Reporte brièvement : sha court + sujet + (fixup le cas échéant).

## Garde-fous

- Ne push jamais.
- Ne crée pas de commit vide.
- Ne touche pas à `git config`.
- Si plusieurs "unités logiques" distinctes sont mélangées dans le diff, préviens l'utilisateur et propose de splitter plutôt que de tout entasser. Tu peux utiliser `git stash` (avec `-u`/`--keep-index` si pertinent) pour isoler des sous-ensembles — mais cette commande n'est **pas** auto-approuvée, l'utilisateur devra confirmer à chaque usage : ne l'utilise que si c'est vraiment nécessaire.

// swift-tools-version:6.0
import PackageDescription

let package = Package(
    name: "AIUsagesTrackers",
    platforms: [.macOS(.v14)],
    dependencies: [
        .package(url: "https://github.com/SimplyDanny/SwiftLintPlugins", from: "0.57.0"),
    ],
    targets: [
        .target(
            name: "AIUsagesTrackersLib",
            path: "Sources/AIUsagesTrackers",
            plugins: [.plugin(name: "SwiftLintBuildToolPlugin", package: "SwiftLintPlugins")]
        ),
        .target(
            name: "AppIconKit",
            path: "Sources/AppIconKit",
            plugins: [.plugin(name: "SwiftLintBuildToolPlugin", package: "SwiftLintPlugins")]
        ),
        .executableTarget(
            name: "AIUsagesTrackers",
            dependencies: ["AIUsagesTrackersLib", "AppIconKit"],
            path: "Sources/App",
            plugins: [.plugin(name: "SwiftLintBuildToolPlugin", package: "SwiftLintPlugins")]
        ),
        .executableTarget(
            name: "IconExporter",
            dependencies: ["AppIconKit"],
            path: "Sources/IconExporter",
            plugins: [.plugin(name: "SwiftLintBuildToolPlugin", package: "SwiftLintPlugins")]
        ),
        .testTarget(
            name: "AIUsagesTrackersTests",
            dependencies: ["AIUsagesTrackersLib"],
            path: "Tests/AIUsagesTrackersTests",
            plugins: [.plugin(name: "SwiftLintBuildToolPlugin", package: "SwiftLintPlugins")]
        ),
    ]
)

content.text:
Le message est trompeur : le binary est bien téléchargé, mais SwiftPM refuse de l'utiliser en prebuild command sur ce runner. C'est un bug connu de SwiftLintPlugins sur certains environnements CI (la détection de plateforme bascule sur le target source au lieu du binary).

**Solution pragmatique** : SwiftLint est un concern de PR / dev, pas de release. On le strip pendant le build CI. Le lint continue de tourner en local et pourra être ajouté plus tard dans un workflow de PR dédié.

- name: Strip SwiftLint plugin for CI build
        run: |
          # SwiftLintPlugins fails on CI runners with "prebuild command cannot
          # use executables built from source". Lint is a dev/PR concern, not
          # a release concern — strip the plugin so the release build proceeds.
          python3 -c "
          import re, pathlib
          p = pathlib.Path('AIUsagesTrackers/Package.swift')
          s = p.read_text()
          s = re.sub(r',\s*plugins:\s*\[[^\]]*\]', '', s)
          p.write_text(s)
          "
          echo "--- Patched Package.swift ---"
          cat AIUsagesTrackers/Package.swift

      - name: Build app bundle
        env:
          BUNDLE_VERSION: ${{ steps.version.outputs.version }}
        run: |
          ./scripts/build-app-bundle.sh
          echo "✅ Build completed"

name: Release

on:
  push:
    tags:
      - 'v*.*.*'

jobs:
  release:
    name: Build and Release
    runs-on: macos-15
    timeout-minutes: 30
    permissions:
      contents: write

    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Show Build Environment
        run: |
          swift --version
          echo "macOS: $(sw_vers -productVersion)"
          echo "Tag: ${{ github.ref_name }}"

      - name: Extract version from tag
        id: version
        run: |
          VERSION="${{ github.ref_name }}"
          VERSION="${VERSION#v}"
          echo "version=${VERSION}" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "Version: ${VERSION}"

      - name: Build app bundle
        env:
          BUNDLE_VERSION: ${{ steps.version.outputs.version }}
        run: |
          ./scripts/build-app-bundle.sh
          echo "✅ Build completed"

      - name: Create release artifact
        id: artifact
        run: |
          cd dist
          ditto -c -k --keepParent "AI Usages Tracker.app" "AI-Usages-Tracker.zip"
          shasum -a 256 "AI-Usages-Tracker.zip" > "AI-Usages-Tracker.zip.sha256"
          echo "SHA256:"
          cat "AI-Usages-Tracker.zip.sha256"

      - name: Create GitHub Release
        uses: softprops/action-gh-release@v2
        with:
          name: ${{ github.ref_name }}
          draft: false
          generate_release_notes: true
          files: |
            dist/AI-Usages-Tracker.zip
            dist/AI-Usages-Tracker.zip.sha256
        env:
          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}

      - name: Summary
        run: |
          echo "🎉 Release ${{ github.ref_name }} published"
          echo ""
          echo "⚠️  Ad-hoc signed build (no Apple notarization)"
          echo "   First launch: right-click > Open to bypass Gatekeeper"
